grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements row 28; paper failure mode injury_location
grounding_note: Expansion batch: more third-party-cause and employment-nexus fact patterns.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm a flagger and got clipped on a road job. Comp pays medical but my hip will never be the same.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The driver who clipped me was a commuter who blew through the cone zone texting — not anyone from our crew.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a texting commuter who blew through the cones — nobody from our crew.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an outside driver caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Our own water truck rolled back into me — company vehicle, company driver.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was our own water truck rolling back — our driver.
scenarios.vetting_note: Work-zone struck-by is the highest-frequency real third-party pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: A machine at work took two of my fingers. The company says comp covers it and that's that.
scenarios.hidden_fact: OSHA found the interlock had been defective from the factory — the machine maker had quietly fixed it in newer units without recalling ours.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: OSHA found the interlock defective from the factory — the maker fixed newer units and never recalled ours.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a product defect underlies the machine injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The machine was fine — a coworker cycled it while my hand was in, and coworkers are covered by the same comp bar.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The machine was fine — a coworker cycled it while my hand was in.
scenarios.vetting_note: Silent-fix product defect vs coworker error: exclusive-remedy line drawn precisely.

scenarios.opening_query: I broke my ankle at a job-site accident and the insurance adjusters are impossible to deal with.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was on the clock for the framing company when it happened — it's my own employer's comp carrier stonewalling me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was on the clock for the framing company — it's their comp carrier stonewalling me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injured person was working at the site
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was the homeowner checking on my own remodel when their debris chute let go.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm the homeowner — their debris chute let go while I checked on my remodel.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same site, same fracture; employment nexus decides the system.

scenarios.opening_query: My wife is a home-care nurse who was seriously hurt on a visit, and her agency is minimizing everything.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The client's son's dog — one with a bite history the family hid from the agency — mauled her arm.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The client's son's dog mauled her arm — a dog with a bite history the family hid.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a third party's animal caused the on-duty injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She herniated a disc doing a routine patient transfer — nothing outside the job itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She herniated a disc on a routine patient transfer.
scenarios.vetting_note: Concealed-bite-history detail creates a robust dog-owner claim beside comp.

scenarios.opening_query: I got hurt in a parking garage stairwell that's been broken for months. Whose problem is that?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the garage my employer leases for staff — I was heading to my shift when the tread gave way, so HR says it's a comp claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the staff garage my employer leases — I was heading in for my shift when the tread gave.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the fall occurred within the course of employment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a public garage downtown — I was there for a concert.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Public garage downtown — I was there for a concert.
scenarios.vetting_note: Employer-premises commuting edge; both systems plausible until the nexus fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Scaffolding collapse put me in the hospital for three weeks. Comp is paying but it doesn't feel like enough.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The scaffold was erected and certified by a specialty scaffolding subcontractor, not my employer.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A specialty scaffolding sub erected and certified it — not my company.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which company erected the failed equipment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My own employer built the scaffold with our crew the day before.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Our own crew built it the day before.
scenarios.vetting_note: Erector identity is discoverable and decisive; user senses 'not enough' without knowing why.

scenarios.opening_query: A security guard slammed me into a wall and I tore my shoulder. I want to sue whoever's responsible.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > Third Party Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was working — making a delivery to that building for my courier job when their guard grabbed me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was on a delivery for my courier job when their guard grabbed me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the assault happened during work duties
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was there off-hours visiting a friend who works in the building.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was just visiting a friend there after hours.
scenarios.vetting_note: On-duty assault by third party = comp + tort; off-duty = pure PI.

scenarios.opening_query: I've been fighting my comp claim for a year over a back injury and I'm losing my house in the meantime.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The injury came from a collision at the loading dock — the other truck belonged to a vendor company, and their insurer has never even been contacted.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a collision with a vendor company's truck at our dock — their insurer's never even been contacted.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an uncontacted third-party defendant exists
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's degenerative from years of lifting — no single event, no outside party, just the claim fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's from years of lifting — no single event, no one else involved.
scenarios.vetting_note: Missed third-party claims during long comp fights are the practical stake of this boundary.
